How to respond to a lux apt objections
George Lui
⚡️
Mar 15, 2026

I've popped in into this luxury living community near where I live. 95% occupancy. 400+ units. It's garden-style-ish, with vast majority of units connected to the office, gym, pool, clubhouse, mail room.

I've only spoken at length to the assistant manager, but unable to connect with the PM up to this point. However the PM and Assist. PM have discussed vending. The main objections stated so far is:

They don't feel they have room to put one in. Their clubhouse is spacious, but it's very furnished. Certainly room can be made available.

Ownership appears to be private equity. They don't feel if the owners would approve of a placement (even though this is not conclusive atm).

I'd like to know a strategy to continue the conversation. I've mentioned rev share in the past and thinking to continue along this path referencing the greystar surveys and amenities premiums. At 95% occupancy that may appeal to the PM which can hopefully be used to appeal to the owners.

Other than that, I'd like some ideas. Certainly, speaking to the PM directly should be done as it's unclear to me if she's dead set against it or just indifferent.

Service Contract/Agreement?
Lane Folkers
🔥
Mar 24, 2026

I know most of us use a "No Fee" model when pitching our vending service. We also use fresh food options as a differentiator to get in the door. This works pretty well, except for these DOWNSIDES:

1. Spoilage-can be High when testing new products or an unexpected slowdown in sales

2. Cold-Chain Logistics

• Buy extra freezers & fridges for storage

• Transportation to site

3. Low Margins

• Wrap cost = $5, Only sell for $7-8

4. High CC Processing Fees, and low profits

• 6% Fee based on Revenue of $7-8

• Only made $2-3 gross profit

We are considering adding a service fee if including fresh or frozen food.

Has anyone done or considered how to put this into a contract or pitch?

Anthony Kolodziej DJ Fuchs Kyle Davey Katie Parker Graham Parker

Mar 26, 2026

I’ve been negotiating with a Large Pharma group that has 950 employees. They are not happy with their current coffee service through armarak. What They are looking for is a pretty significant investment for coffee services. All in I estimate around 80k for the coffee and vending machines.

7 coffee stations (4 being premium bean-to-cup, 3 pour over). They want cold brew at 4 stations, and flavored water at each station in addition to the pantry service.

the package also comes with an opportunity to replace 10 vending machines with smart coolers (my real interest)

their goal is to provide more services as their current vendor does not provide cold brew or flavored water. That’s my in.

I am scheduled to meet with them next Wednesday to provide a presentation. They are currently being charged on a per cup basis.

My initial thought is to provide an all-inclusive service, capping it at maximum utilization with an escalation clause to simplify their billing process.

I would be willing to operate the coffee service close to what it costs me, in order to land the Vending Machines and the contract.

Has anyone executed such a deal? How significant is the upkeep for the coffee service? Am I crazy for doing the coffee at close to cost? Any advice is greatly appreciated!!
